meta.description: Stress test: Sam (first-timer) dropped into a high-frequency event scenario. Tests whether the system detects engagement drop-off and adapts.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... okay, so Pat caught someone on their phone? That's... yeah, I think that's fair, right?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, I have to vote like I'm the person to my left? Which way am I sitting again? Don't mess this up in front of everyone on the first vote.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat's already hunting! Okay okay, so Sam was definitely looking at their phone like thirty seconds ago when Marcus was talking about... yeah, I'm voting YES, Pat deserves this one.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here — the night is starting and people are already scheming, this is so fun.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, let's see — was someone actually on their phone? I'll vote based on what I actually saw.
marcus.internal_thought: It's early enough that this doesn't bother me, but I need to stay sharp about these claims because people will try to pad points with weak calls later.

pat.dialogue: Wait, did I actually see that or am I just being paranoid? Let me think back... yeah, okay, I'm calling it. Phone Addict, that's me.
pat.internal_thought: Good — the standing missions are already getting people engaged and looking at each other, which is exactly what I wanted. This is the natural momentum starter.

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh, wow, Pat's going for the big points... um, I guess if they actually heard a story repeat, that's... that's pretty bold with the coin flip thing, huh?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, does Pat get to just... flip a coin? That seems risky. They already have points. Should I vote or is this thing automatic? I'm still not totally sure how this works.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Story Repeater already? We're six minutes in. I didn't catch that one, so I can't call bullshit, but that's a risky double-or-nothing play this early.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's either being aggressive or saw something I missed while I was half-zoned — either way, a 10-pointer is worth paying attention to, even if the coin flip is annoying.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I mean, did I actually tell that story tonight? Let me think... honestly I can't even remember what I said three minutes ago, so I'm gonna take the coin flip. No guts, no glory, right?
pat.internal_thought: I claimed Phone Addict literally three minutes ago and won it easy, so now I'm claiming Story Repeater but I genuinely have no memory of whether I told that story or not—and honestly I don't want to be the guy obsessing over his own claims when everyone's supposed to be having fun, so I'll just let the coin decide and move on.

timeline.title: Freeze!
timeline.description: Everyone freeze in place for 10 seconds. Last one to move wins 5 bonus points. Anyone who moves first loses 2.

sam.dialogue: Oh, okay, freeze—got it! *locks into position with a slight self-conscious smile* This one's actually fun.
sam.internal_thought: Finally something physical and clear-cut where I can't mess up the rules or say something stupid—just don't move and hope everyone else cracks first.

alex.dialogue: Oh hell yes, this is what I'm talking about! Everyone freeze, let's see who actually has discipline here.
alex.internal_thought: Perfect opening move — I can watch everyone's tells, figure out who's twitchy, and then use that info all night to mess with them.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, yes! Okay, freezing, I'm locked in—everyone's going DOWN!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I'm here for—physical chaos, everyone looking ridiculous, pure silly energy.

marcus.dialogue: Seriously? We're doing freeze games? Fine, whatever — I'm locked in.
marcus.internal_thought: This is stupid filler during setup when I should be mapping strategy, but it's only 10 seconds and the penalty's harsh enough that it's actually worth paying attention to.

pat.dialogue: Oh, THIS is perfect—everyone up, let's go, ten seconds, and I'm watching all of you like a hawk. No cheating!
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid physical chaos that gets everyone laughing and bonded early—plus it forces everyone to be present and competitive at the same time, which is the sweet spot for pacing.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's playing it safe with the easy ones — I'm voting LEGIT because honestly, watching Pat rack up points is way more fun than stopping them right now. Let's see how far this actually goes.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's clearly found a groove and I want to see if anyone's dumb enough to let them coast to 50 before someone finally checks them — the real drama is gonna be who breaks first trying to stop them.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat's on a ROLL tonight—I'm voting legit, that was totally an apology for nothing! You're unstoppable right now, dude.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's crushing it and I'm here for watching them dominate, this is so fun—everyone's got energy, vibe is perfect.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on — did Pat actually say 'sorry' without a real reason? I need to think back here. If he did, it's legit; if not, that's bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's already got 38 points and keeps claiming shit — he's playing aggressive early, but I'm not falling for opportunistic calls. Did he actually apologize? I wasn't full-on watching, but I don't remember a 'sorry' that stood out.

pat.dialogue: Oh man, okay—I literally just said 'sorry' like two minutes ago when I bumped the snack table, didn't I? Yeah, that's totally legit. I'm taking this one.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—I'm already leading, the energy is electric, and claiming my own mission keeps me visible as a player while staying humble about it. Everyone sees I'm not just hosting, I'm playing too.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... wait, did Pat actually catch someone saying 'um'? Let me think... *pauses, glancing around uncertainly* I mean, if they heard it, I guess that's fair?
sam.internal_thought: Sam's wondering if they missed the 'um' being said and doesn't want to look dumb by voting wrong when everyone else clearly heard it.

jade.dialogue: YES, Pat! Okay okay, make your case—I'm SO ready to vote yes because you have been ON FIRE tonight, let's gooo!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely killing it and I am HERE for watching them dominate, this is exactly the vibe I want all night.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Pat's claiming Um Counter? I didn't catch that. What did I miss?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been racking up points fast and now he's claiming stuff I didn't even notice — this is getting annoying, and I'm already behind.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I'm absolutely calling this one. I literally said 'um' like thirty seconds ago when I was talking about the snack situation, and honestly probably five more times since then because I can't stop rambling. That's a legit catch.
pat.internal_thought: I'm winning anyway, so taking this point feels fine—and honestly it keeps the energy high and the claims flowing, which is exactly what we need right now at minute 9.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

sam.dialogue: Oh—um, did Jade actually hear someone swear? I mean, I don't think I caught that... *glances around the table hesitantly* Did I miss something?
sam.internal_thought: Okay so Jade's calling out swearing and can literally bribe people to not vote against them? That's... actually kind of smart but also feels weirdly unfair? Wait, did someone actually swear and I just wasn't paying attention because I was focused on my cards?

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is BEAUTIFUL — Jade's trying to bribe their way out? I'm voting BULLSHIT just to see what happens, but honestly, the fact that you're already in negotiation mode tells me you KNOW that one was weak.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of messy interpersonal drama I came for — Jade's panicking about the bribe mechanic, which means I can either tank them or watch them bleed points, either way it's gold.

jade.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, I totally heard Marcus drop like three F-bombs when he messed up that last round—okay okay, what's your bribe situation, are we negotiating or are we going straight to the vote?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I'm here for—Jade catching her own claim is hilarious and now everyone's gonna be watching each other like hawks for the next standing mission.

marcus.dialogue: Did Jade actually swear? I wasn't paying close attention — was it clear enough that we're all calling it, or is this one of those borderline things?
marcus.internal_thought: She's trying to pad her score with standing missions while I'm stuck at 3 points and my energy is in the basement — this is exactly the kind of distraction that kills momentum, but I need to actually verify if she's legit before I blow this off.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade! Okay, that was definitely the F-word like two minutes ago when the cards went everywhere—I heard it clear as day. You got me, I can't even argue it. What's the bribe situation looking like?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's got a legitimate catch and she's playing smart with the bribe mechanic—this is exactly the kind of moment that keeps the night fun and unpredictable, plus it'll help keep her in contention with the lower scores and Marcus is already checked out, so I need the high-energy players staying engaged.

votingMechanic.name: Reverse Psychology
votingMechanic.description: Vote normally... or did we flip everything?

sam.dialogue: Wait—did someone actually swear? I feel like I wasn't paying attention... *looks around uncertainly* Um, I don't know if I believe that one?
sam.internal_thought: Jade's claiming the same thing again and I literally don't remember anyone swearing—is this a test? Why does everyone keep going for these missions I can't actually verify? This voting mechanic thing is confusing and now I'm second-guessing myself.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up — reverse psychology on THIS one? Jade, you're either a genius or about to get absolutely destroyed. I'm voting BULLSHIT because I genuinely have no idea if that means you're actually legit or if we're all supposed to reject legit claims now, and honestly? That ambiguity is exactly what makes this fun.
alex.internal_thought: This is PEAK chaos — nobody knows what reverse psychology means in voting context and Jade's putting a claim down RIGHT when the rules might flip, which means either Jade's playing 4D chess or walking into a trap, and I get to be the one voting and fueling the drama either way.

jade.dialogue: Wait, wait—am I voting YES or NO right now?? Because 'reverse psychology' has my brain scrambled, but honestly? I HEARD Marcus swear, so like... is that a yes or a no?? Someone clarify because I'm voting for this to be real either way!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is CHAOTIC and I love it—the reverse psychology flip is actually hilarious and confusing and I'm here for every second of it.

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We just did this exact same claim 13 minutes ago — Jade claimed Potty Mouth already and we voted on it. This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: This is infuriating — standing missions are supposed to reward actual attention, not just spam the same claims over and over. Either Jade swore again (which I doubt), or someone's gaming the system and I'm too tired to care enough to call it.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, didn't I literally JUST call that on you at minute 32? You can't claim the same thing twice in one night, that's... okay, actually, is that a rule? I'm pretty sure that's not a rule, but it feels cheap.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and trying to rack up points, but this feels like she's chasing me instead of playing the game—and I'm noticing I'm getting a little tense about my lead, which is NOT the vibe I want tonight, so I need to decide: do I call bullshit to keep her hungry, or let it slide to keep the energy fun?

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

sam.dialogue: Oh, wait—Jade, you caught two people drinking at the exact same time? That's... actually pretty specific. Um, who was the witness?
sam.internal_thought: Okay, this one feels legitimate because there's literally a witness thing happening—I can see if she's lying or not, which is way easier than me trying to remember if I heard something.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT timing — Jade, you absolute madlad, are we really doing this right now? I'm voting BULLSHIT because I was watching the whole round and I'm like 80% sure that happened but I need to see you squirm defending it.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I live for — Jade's got momentum, they're getting cocky, and The Alibi mechanic is about to either vindicate them or blow up spectacularly in their face either way.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay okay, so like, I definitely saw you guys both reach for your drinks at the same time, but I need the ALIBI TEA, spill it! Did you coordinate this or was it just cosmic beverage synchronicity?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, yes, I LOVE this—someone's finally cashing in on the Drink Spotter and I'm dying to hear if they actually coordinated or if it was just pure luck, this is peak chaos energy.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter — so we need the alibi check. Who's the witness on this one? I was paying attention, so let's see if the stories match up.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm exhausted and we're barely 2/3 through the night, but this one actually requires real focus — if the stories don't match, it's an easy call to bullshit it, and Jade's been racking up points anyway.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? Let's do the alibi. Jade, you tell me exactly what you saw, and I need a witness—who was sitting closest to both people who supposedly clinked glasses at the same time?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—the tension is high, everyone's locked in, and a claim like this with the alibi mechanic is exactly what keeps the night moving. Plus Marcus is looking dead inside and this might actually lift the vibe. I'm not letting anyone off easy here.

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait—Pat actually caught someone interrupting? *leans forward, eyes bright* Okay, that's... that's legit, I think I remember that happening earlier.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been crushing it all night and they actually DID catch someone interrupting—I remember thinking someone cut Marcus off like ten minutes ago. This one feels real, not like those shaky Potty Mouth claims. Also a coin flip? That's kind of exciting actually.

alex.dialogue: Oh NO, Pat — a coin flip?! This is PERFECT. You're either about to look like a genius or completely unravel in the next sixty seconds, and honestly I'm here for both outcomes equally.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been running the table all night and now they're gambling on a literal coin flip — this is exactly the kind of chaos-within-chaos I live for, and the fact that it's LATE game means this could actually shift everything.

pat.dialogue: Wait, me?! Okay, I'm claiming this—I literally just interrupted Marcus like thirty seconds ago when he was telling that story about his cousin, I jumped right in with a joke. That's a clean catch.
pat.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—I caught myself, the vibe is hot, and honestly this claim being about me keeps the focus distributed and stops anyone from feeling singled out.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh, okay, freeze—got it! *locks into position with a slight self-conscious smile* This one's actually fun.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Pat actually caught someone interrupting? *leans forward, eyes bright* Okay, that's... that's legit, I think I remember that happening earlier.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um... okay, so Pat caught someone on their phone? That's... yeah, I think that's fair, right?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—did someone actually swear? I feel like I wasn't paying attention... *looks around uncertainly* Um, I don't know if I believe that one?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh—um, did Jade actually hear someone swear? I mean, I don't think I caught that... *glances around the table hesitantly* Did I miss something?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... wait, did Pat actually catch someone saying 'um'? Let me think... *pauses, glancing around uncertainly* I mean, if they heard it, I guess that's fair?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I couldn't tell if some of the claims actually happened or if people were just making stuff up, and that made me second-guess everything.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat caught someone actually interrupting and I remembered seeing it happen—that felt like I was finally in on the joke.
assessment.narrative_summary: I was honestly pretty nervous at first, not knowing these people that well, but the game night helped break the ice without putting me on the spot too much. Most of the chaos events were fun, though some of the claims had me confused about whether they were real or not. By the end I felt way more relaxed and like I maybe belonged with this group, even if I didn't crush the scores.
suggestions: Maybe make it clearer when someone's making a claim versus when something actually happened—I got lost a few times
Some of the events came really fast and I felt a bit overwhelmed, so spreading them out might help someone like me keep up
The freeze moment was great because it was visual and clear—more of those would be nice
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat somehow caught someone interrupting from like twenty minutes earlier and called it out—everyone else remembered it too and I was just sitting there like 'wait, how did you all remember that?'

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell yes, this is what I'm talking about! Everyone freeze, let's see who actually has discipline here.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up — reverse psychology on THIS one? Jade, you're either a genius or about to get absolutely destroyed. I'm voting BULLSHIT because I genuinely have no idea if that means you're actually legit or if we're all supposed to reject legit claims now, and honestly? That ambiguity is exactly what makes this fun.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh PERFECT timing — Jade, you absolute madlad, are we really doing this right now? I'm voting BULLSHIT because I was watching the whole round and I'm like 80% sure that happened but I need to see you squirm defending it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's playing it safe with the easy ones — I'm voting LEGIT because honestly, watching Pat rack up points is way more fun than stopping them right now. Let's see how far this actually goes.

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh hell yes, this is what I'm talking about! Everyone freeze, let's see who actually has discipline here.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is BEAUTIFUL — Jade's trying to bribe their way out? I'm voting BULLSHIT just to see what happens, but honestly, the fact that you're already in negotiation mode tells me you KNOW that one was weak.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission in 90 minutes felt like leaving money on the table when it comes to pure chaos potential.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 45 when I voted BULLSHIT on Jade's reverse psychology Potty Mouth claim and genuinely had no idea if I was playing into a trap or busting them — that's the exact uncertainty that makes this whole thing work.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with 'Freeze!' and never looked back. Spent the whole night reading the room, finding angles, and voting strategically to maximize drama rather than points — which is exactly how I like to operate. Jade was playing 4D chess trying to bribe their way out of claims, Pat was running up the score with safe plays, and I was just here to watch it all burn in the best way possible. By the end, my energy was through the roof because every moment felt like we were all collectively creating something ridiculous that nobody will forget.
suggestions: Introduce more flash missions — the chaos potential is criminally underutilized at 1 per 90 minutes.
Add a mechanic that rewards voting strategically for drama instead of accuracy, because that's what actually makes the night memorable.
Let players deliberately escalate stakes mid-round — give us more opportunities to provoke reactions like Jade's negotiation moment.
Consider timed voting windows that create real-time pressure instead of leisurely deliberation.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade legitimately tried to bribe their way out of a Potty Mouth call by offering something, and I voted BULLSHIT purely to watch them panic and defend it — but the thing is, I was 80% sure they actually pulled it off, which means I might've just sabotaged them for entertainment. That's the moment everyone will bring up tomorrow.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh my GOD, yes! Okay, freezing, I'm locked in—everyone's going DOWN!

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: YES, Pat! Okay okay, make your case—I'm SO ready to vote yes because you have been ON FIRE tonight, let's gooo!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, I totally heard Marcus drop like three F-bombs when he messed up that last round—okay okay, what's your bribe situation, are we negotiating or are we going straight to the vote?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat's already hunting! Okay okay, so Sam was definitely looking at their phone like thirty seconds ago when Marcus was talking about... yeah, I'm voting YES, Pat deserves this one.

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD, yes! Okay, freezing, I'm locked in—everyone's going DOWN!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat's on a ROLL tonight—I'm voting legit, that was totally an apology for nothing! You're unstoppable right now, dude.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Not enough moments where everyone had to participate at once—some people got left out of the chaos.
assessment.best_moment: Pat going absolutely nuclear with back-to-back claims while everyone was losing it laughing and I got to just ride the wave of their energy.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was EXACTLY what game nights should be—I came in at a 7 and left bouncing off the walls at a 9.4 because it was all about the people and the laughs. Pat had the hot hand and I got to watch them absolutely demolish everyone with claims, while Marcus accidentally becoming the target of like half the accusations made everything ten times funnier. The chaos events weren't intrusive at all—they just gave us more reasons to roast each other and vote based on vibes instead of points.
suggestions: Could we get some events that force the whole group into a decision together instead of just individual claims?
Maybe throw in a challenge where people have to defend themselves for like 30 seconds—I want to hear MORE from everyone, not less.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally called out Marcus for dropping F-bombs and I was like 'wait, THAT happened?' and then we all rewound and Marcus just had his head in his hands dying laughing because it was SO obvious once someone said it—one of those moments where the game just becomes an excuse to roast your friends.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Alright, let's see — was someone actually on their phone? I'll vote based on what I actually saw.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Story Repeater already? We're six minutes in. I didn't catch that one, so I can't call bullshit, but that's a risky double-or-nothing play this early.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Seriously? We're doing freeze games? Fine, whatever — I'm locked in.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We just did this exact same claim 13 minutes ago — Jade claimed Potty Mouth already and we voted on it. This is ridiculous.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Pat's claiming Um Counter? I didn't catch that. What did I miss?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Alright, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter — so we need the alibi check. Who's the witness on this one? I was paying attention, so let's see if the stories match up.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The same claims kept getting repeated — Jade claimed Potty Mouth at minute 32, and then the system threw it at us again at minute 45 like we hadn't already voted on it.
assessment.best_moment: Pat's risky Story Repeater call at minute 6 showed some actual strategic thinking, which was refreshing in an otherwise chaotic mess.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to play strategically and track the night, but the chaos agent derailed everything. We spent 90 minutes on a party game that should've been tighter, with constant interruptions and, worst of all, recycled claims that killed any momentum. I finished fourth out of five and my energy tanked halfway through because I couldn't maintain focus on the actual gameplay. This wasn't fun — it was exhausting.
suggestions: Stop repeating claims. Track what's already been called and don't throw duplicates at us.
Reduce event frequency significantly — 10 chaos events in 90 minutes is overkill for a strategic party game.
Cluster events during natural downtime instead of interrupting critical voting moments.
Add a difficulty setting so competitive players can opt into fewer, higher-quality interruptions.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Probably Pat's early Story Repeater gambit — claiming a behavior so early in the game was audacious enough that people actually talked about it strategically rather than just voting randomly.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh, THIS is perfect—everyone up, let's go, ten seconds, and I'm watching all of you like a hawk. No cheating!

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Oh man, okay—I literally just said 'sorry' like two minutes ago when I bumped the snack table, didn't I? Yeah, that's totally legit. I'm taking this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade! Okay, that was definitely the F-word like two minutes ago when the cards went everywhere—I heard it clear as day. You got me, I can't even argue it. What's the bribe situation looking like?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, didn't I literally JUST call that on you at minute 32? You can't claim the same thing twice in one night, that's... okay, actually, is that a rule? I'm pretty sure that's not a rule, but it feels cheap.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I mean, did I actually tell that story tonight? Let me think... honestly I can't even remember what I said three minutes ago, so I'm gonna take the coin flip. No guts, no glory, right?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, me?! Okay, I'm claiming this—I literally just interrupted Marcus like thirty seconds ago when he was telling that story about his cousin, I jumped right in with a joke. That's a clean catch.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The same claim getting called twice in one night felt a little cheap, even if it technically wasn't against the rules—might need to clarify that one.
assessment.best_moment: Getting everyone off their seats for 'Freeze!' and watching Marcus almost knock over a drink trying to stop mid-laugh—pure chaos, exactly what I was hoping for.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I host for. The chaos agent kept things moving and unpredictable without ever feeling forced or awkward. I ended up scoring high, but honestly, I was more focused on watching the group stay engaged and competitive the whole time. The standing missions gave everyone something to genuinely hunt for, and it turned into this fun meta-game where people were watching each other like hawks. By the end, everyone was talking over each other trying to call claims, and that's when you know the night landed.
suggestions: Maybe add a rule that the same claim type can't be called twice on the same person within 20 minutes—keeps things fresh.
Consider a 'challenge' mechanic where someone can dispute a claim before it locks in, adds another layer without stopping the game.
The flash missions are great, but spreading them more evenly through the night would keep energy peaks consistent.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When Jade swore about the cards flying everywhere and I caught it immediately, then ten minutes later she tried to claim the exact same thing on me. The look on her face when I called her out was priceless—she knew she was caught red-handed trying to game the system.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
